title: Amortized cost of binary counter increments
statement_informal: Performing n increments on a binary counter, starting from empty, costs at most 2n bit flips (amortized O(1) per increment).

cost_model.notes: Aggregate/amortized bound, proved with the potential = popcount: total flips + ones(state) = 2n.

references: Cormen et al., Introduction to Algorithms — amortized analysis of the binary counter (fact only)
